skill.name: react-state-flows
skill.description: Guidance for designing, implementing, testing, and documenting complex multi-step state flows in React and React Native applications.
skill.summary: Build reliable React state flows with explicit steps, validations, error states, and integration tests.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives in markdown diagrams, TypeScript examples, and descriptive headings. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found in the single scanned file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Backticks in Markdown and TypeScript Examples
low_findings.description: The external command findings are caused by markdown code fences and TypeScript template literals inside documentation examples. They do not execute Ruby, shell commands, or subprocesses.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are documentation fences, template strings, logging examples, and error messages. No executable shell, Ruby backtick evaluation, or command runner is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings do not identify cryptographic operations. The referenced lines contain front matter, a test description, and a checklist heading.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context shows no hashing, encryption, key generation, or dependency on weak algorithms. The scanner appears to have matched generic text, not security code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are caused by ordinary prose and a React state update example. They do not inspect the host system or collect environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced text discusses implicit flow states and sets an order completion state in a React example. There is no uname, whoami, filesystem inventory, or host probing behavior.

content.value_statement: Complex React flows often fail because state changes, async steps, and errors are handled in separate places. This skill helps teams model explicit transitions, validate each step, and test full user flows.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to model React workflows as explicit states and transitions.
Shows how to sequence async flow steps with preconditions and postconditions.
Provides TypeScript examples for flow objects and step validation.
Demonstrates UI state tracking for loading, error, and completion states.
Recommends integration tests that verify happy paths and failure paths.
Suggests when to use XState instead of a simple explicit flow.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a reusable library or installed package.
It does not include complete production checkout, payment, or order APIs.
It assumes the user can adapt examples to their chosen React state library.
It does not replace formal state machine tooling for highly complex workflows.

use_cases.title: Design Checkout Flow Logic
use_cases.description: Create an explicit checkout sequence with validation, payment, confirmation, and error handling states.

use_cases.title: Debug Async Ordering Bugs
use_cases.description: Review a multi-step React workflow and identify missing awaits, invalid transitions, or absent postcondition checks.

use_cases.title: Plan Flow Coverage
use_cases.description: Define integration tests that exercise the complete flow and failures at each important step.

prompt_templates.title: Map a Basic Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use react-state-flows to map my React flow into states, transitions, preconditions, postconditions, and error states.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Step Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use react-state-flows to turn this async React workflow into explicit ordered steps with validation after each step.

prompt_templates.title: Review Flow State UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Use react-state-flows to review how my component exposes loading, error, retry, and completion states to users.

prompt_templates.title: Design Failure Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use react-state-flows to create an integration test plan for the happy path and each failure path in this flow.

output_examples.input: A checkout process validates a cart, charges a card, creates an order, and shows confirmation.
output: A state list covering idle, validating, payment, confirming, complete, and error.
A transition checklist with required preconditions and postconditions for each step.
A test plan for successful checkout and payment failure.

output_examples.input: A React Native onboarding flow saves profile data, uploads an avatar, and requests notification permission.
output: A recommended flow object with named steps and validation points.
UI state guidance for progress, retry, cancellation, and final success.
Notes on when the flow may need a formal state machine.

best_practices: Document every important state before writing the async implementation.
Validate postconditions after each step, especially after external API calls.
Test the complete flow and at least one failure case for each critical step.
anti_patterns: Do not spread one workflow across unrelated components without a clear transition model.
Do not update UI state only at the end of a long async operation.
Do not rely on unit tests alone for workflows with dependent async steps.

faq.question: Does this skill require XState?
faq.answer: No. It teaches explicit flow design first and suggests XState only for more complex state machines.

faq.question: Can it help with React Native?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance applies to React web and React Native flows with async steps and UI feedback.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete app?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns, examples, and review guidance that users adapt to their app.

faq.question: What state libraries does it assume?
faq.answer: The examples use store-style state access, but the concepts work with Zustand, Redux, Context, or local state.

faq.question: Can it find missing await bugs?
faq.answer: It can guide review of async ordering and highlight where awaited steps and validations should exist.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation examples only and no executable command, network, or credential access behavior.
